title: Duke dorming is wild... #dormlife #collegelife #dormtour #dukeuniversity #dormroom #collegememories
body: At my school, we filled out surveys so they could match us with compatible roommates. I filled in that I was quiet, clean, and went to bed early. So they matched me with a loud party girl who never cleaned up after herself and often had people over who went through my stuff and laid on my bed. She also never spoke 3 words to me even when I tried to introduce myself. It was the most awful time of my life. They matched me with the polar opposite.

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body: Man, I remember having a 4.2 GPA and a 33 on the ACT, along with extracurriculars, and still not being accepted into Ivys. I’m happy though, because I went to a state school and ended up with so many opportunities to explore, without constantly feeling pressured to compete with my peers!

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body: For y’all in the comments who are confused about the gpas: every school weighs them differently. Most do a 5.0 scale, but there are also some that do 6.0, or even rarer ones that go higher than that. Colleges view your gpa based on YOUR school’s scale, so while 5.2 might sound insane, you have to consider the fact that it’s probably on a 6.0.

author: @Clizzy1000

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body:
Love that these kids can get where they are. I want them to be appreciative of the home life they had. I hope they understand that many of their peers, did not grow up in loving happy homes where you can concentrate on studies. Yes, a terrible homelife can spur some people to go hard. But also, it is a huge barrier for others. As an adult, who has made a decent life for himself, my life would have been so different had I had the parental support and upbringing in the beginning. My entire first 30 years were spent running away and escpaing from trauma, anger, and parents who had so much trauma themselves. This is why the underdog story works so well. If you are successful with so many barriers in your way, I can relate.

To this day, family say, he was a video game junkie and anti-social. No. No. I was trying to escape my home life. I found myself lost in Final Fantasy IV, with the characters, I fell in love with the fake women, I dreamed of people just loving me for protecting them. I went to school, I was scared of people because my parents never taught me how to be social. I never had friends over, I was so embarrassed they'd see my dad passed out on pain killers or fighting with my mom. My best friend was getting ready for college at 17 and asking me what I was going to do. All I could say was, I guess community college. I was preparing to move out on my own with a job that paid $8 an hour. And my life was not bad. There are kids who were beaten and sexually abused. There are so many people wth pain and trauma. Do your best. And for those who didn't... So glad you didn't have to experience it. Own it. Try to get some experience from others. Respect them. Love them. Learn from them. Be always your best version.
author: @novellanightmares

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body: For everyone thinking about the GPAs they said, they are probably out of varying scales and also weighted. Most colleges calculate your gpa on their scale anyways and certain colleges use unweighted GPA(no honors or AP/IB)

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body:
2.8 GPA
1480 SAT
34 ACT
No extracurricular activities
No AP tests
Lived in a "middle class" household
Worked full time as an apprentice contractor doing electrical and plumbing at 18.
Went to Community college cause 2 years free tuition was a no brainer, stayed for 5 because I was still working full time to make ends meet.
Transferred to State college for 2026 and at the same time became a fully licensed freelance contractor cause why tf not.

Sure, my life pre college was garbage, but at least I made it a life worth living. Heck, I make $6k a month on average, with a peak of $9k just last month. Yes I plan to use most of it for college, but I've always put a percentage of my money into a high yield savings account, Dividend stocks, and Growth stocks. Parents still don't like my choices, but hey, If someone's pulling the strings it better be me.
author: @kennethle3558

title: How Duke Students Got Into Duke! #college #collegeadmissions #duke
body: To anyone wondering, colleges view weighted GPA as a 4.0. Anything above a 4.0 is just used by the high school to determine class rank but colleges don’t actually look at that.

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I went to Cornell and made a ton of important connections and when I applied to grad school (medical school) a ton of my interviewers were Cornell alum and we were able to connect and just hit off immediately due to the connection.  I eventually went to NYU, where a solid 10% of my classmates were Cornell alum and had a fantastic time.  I’m now starting my academic career in medicine on the west coast, where, again I run into and hit it off with a gaggle of former Cornell grads and have instant connection.  Cornell changed my life, when I can, I will donate back to my Alma mater

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I attend Cornell, and there are countless aspects of my school that I genuinely value, including numerous activities like sports, games, club events and so on. I never feel "stuck" inside the walls of Cornell - In fact, I only wish I could have so much time to explore everything that my school has to offer. I never experience any form of inferiority complex because of my association with this institution. You mentioned Andy Bernard and I must confess that I didn't know who he was until you brought him up, so any reference to him is quite irrelevant. It's essential to recognize that allowing one individual to shape your perception of an entire group is a flawed approach. Not everyone gets admitted into a competitive school because of a good SAT score. Instances of legacy admissions are present in various prestigious institutions, including Harvard and Yale, and drawing broad conclusions based on such factors is, in essence, an immature perspective. Let's focus on appreciating the unique qualities of each academic institution without letting isolated incidents overshadow the collective experiences of its diverse student body.
author: @moreblessingmushohwe6506

title: *WATCH THIS BEFORE PICKING DUKE* | the cons of duke university FT @sgfvann
body:
The cons:

-Forced 3 year on-campus housing (pre-COVID)
-Study abroad opportunities are not great with Duke--especially if you're in STEM
-Financial aid sucks if you're middle class (I've had to argue every year at Duke to give me more aid)
-Food plans are either too small or too large; food plans force unhealthy eating habits
-Administration is pretty white and racist--it is a private school in the South lol
-School is quite unwelcoming to Palestinians in particular; if a Palestinian advocates they're quick to be labeled as anti-semitic. In fact, this point actually compels me to say if you're Palestinian I'd consider how much of a support group you need before deciding to attend Duke cause you're not gonna get much there.
-Student body is quite self-segregated
-Parking is meh but I don't think it's that much worse from other schools
-Lot of the professors are meh too--they're top researchers and amazing in their field but teaching is not their top priority and some are only doing it cause they're forced too if they don't receive enough grants so it really translates into the quality of teaching
-I kinda wish the student body had more international students; I often found them the most interesting and culturally-aware compared to the typical American college student

Duke '21 Alumnus
author: @You-dh1du

title: Duke Dumont & Gorgon City live at Café Mambo for Radio 1 in Ibiza 2017
body: Someone in the audience closer, a little closer, to our age (62 yrs). Great to see some older, grey hair etc and being in the groove. We love this music soooo much, we intend to move to this island of our dreams. As Tritonal say it: Now or Never :) Saw Duke Dumont twice (1x at EDC) and Gorgon City once; great team. Thank you!

title: Day in the Life of a Law Student
body:
"You grab three cups of coffee to realize your professor isn't there." Why is this so accurate on one of my Law subjects on college? Like literally... he'll just show up twice a month and on the day of exams, he always didn't show up but one of his subordinates. And by subordinates... he owns a law firm so he substitute one of his partners. 😂

PS (Accountancy course in our country have 4 laws as subjects)
author: @07kamichamakarin

title: Day in the Life of a Law Student
body: You'll watch an entire Netflix show even when the first episodes are slow and boring just because someone told you "it gets better." But what if you looked at your goals like that and watched your life get better instead?

title: If Yale were honest #yale #college #collegeadmissions
body:
Not to mention Yale, is essentially a big reason why new haven, which is literally where I live is so poor. They do absolutely nothing for the city that they take up so much of. They're the reason that rent is so astronomical here. As people want luxury apartments. They keep building more luxury apartments here, instead of actually building housing for the majority of people who live here.

You are so right about how they act about harvard. You should see how it is during a Harvard / Yale game, there are literally like a bunch of fools.

title: If Yale were honest #yale #college #collegeadmissions

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: I was born in New York, lived there for my pre K years, still have family there and genuinely do not understand why people think New York is so unfriendly. I always have the best conversations and meet nice people when I go back. Sure, there are the wack a doos, but you can find those people all sorts of places. New Yorkers are great.
author: @user-jv5pp8pv9l

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: New York City is full of kindness, camaraderie, and love. Being surrounded by millions of people and being forced to interact thanks to the lack of cars is a recipe for empathy throughput the city. Ofc, there are shitty people everywhere, but it is so easy to make friends in NYC.
author: @sragvit8014

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: When I visited NY, everyone was not only friendly, but proactively helpful. Maybe that was a byproduct of the pandemic times and they were just happy for whatever tourism they could get, but I still question the stereotype of unfriendly New Yorkers.
author: @ThirdLawPair

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: My daughter just graduated from Columbia — I'd say this was pretty accurate ( especially about NYC being  way more their social focus than anything on campus ) )Although she actually appreciated and enjoyed the core curriculum

body: Everyone I know who went to Colombia undergrad was taking 6 classes a semester instead of the normal 4 like almost every other college.

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: My brother went to Columbia (a long time ago) and his main issue with going there was how expensive NYC was.  I also remember the complete lack of school spirit, etc.. much more likely to see people wearing the baseball hats of other colleges and not Columbia's.  That being said- phenomenal school.

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: I got into Columbia and Princeton.  The worst thing those two schools did was fly me out for recruitment.  I probably would have picked them if I didn't know exactly how hellish life would be on those campuses.
author: @swicheroo1

title: If Columbia were honest #columbia #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions
body: I was on holidays in New York in 2019. It was the friendliest place I've ever been. I was lost in Brooklyn. Three people stopped to help me, one offered to walk me to my AirBnb. I almost tripped near Central Park and this lady looked at me and said, "I got chu". My international card didn't work at a cafe and the lady still told me to take my food. It was the nicest city I've ever been!

title: The Biggest Stereotype of Duke Students...
body:
I attended a private school in college. The rest is public school. I remember when we were having our games in school and our opponents were from different schools, which mostly consisted of public schools, including my past alma mater in high school. One of my friends mentions how the school's students always smell like the sun and how ugly the uniform is—a non-stop insult per se. Then all of them agreed with her. At that time, I was shocked but proudly said that I was from that school. As soon as I said that, all of them got quiet.

P.s. My former alma mater won all the games.
author: @lyneedsmoraxxx

title: The Biggest Stereotype of Duke Students...
body: I graduated from a private high school and can confirm that the stereotype are true especially between your colleagues when they ask where you go and you told them. It sometimes makes me really uncomfortable when they started behaving differently.

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: I like Brown, the idea of Brown and how it operates. I was accepted by Brown for graduate school, but I chose Stanford instead. I’ll never know if I made the right decision.

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: When I was at Brown in the 90s, we had the lowest GPA in the Ivies, because it seemed it didn't matter if you worked hard in a class or slacked, you got a B either way... I guess I went there at the wrong time!
author: @hyoyoon

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: brown was always my dream school simply because i thought they were adorbale for allowing you to supplement your app with a video or even art portfolio. it meant a lot to me as a creative kid, but in the end i never got to apply bc my parents didn’t want me to go out of state :(
author: @pieceaisa5046

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: Brown feels like the only Ivy league that still feels like an Ivy league. Genuine focus on the liberal arts and actually preparing students to think rather than just STEM.
author: @ratdadsta

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: Graduated from Brown last year with a CS degree, like any college, it can be as hard as you want to make it. The top commenter said he has never been stressed a day, I was stressed even before starting school and the only period of time I wasn’t stressed was after my senior thesis was submitted (May of senior year 😢)
author: @adeliad5242

title: if Brown were honest #ivyleague #college #collegeadmissions #brown #harvard #stanford
body: bro my classmate’s sister got into brown but then they reversed their decision bc she was homeschooled even though she didn’t hide it from them and was completely upfront about it 💀
